sentence: The _____ of the new building took two years.
hint: Think about the noun form with -tion
explanation: CONSTRUCT → construction (suffix -tion: the process of constructing)

sentence: She received an _____ to the party.
explanation: INVITE → invitation (suffix -tion: drop -e, add -ation)

sentence: I need more _____ about the course.
hint: The noun form meaning facts or details
explanation: INFORM → information (suffix -ation: the act of informing)

sentence: His _____ of the problem was very clear.
explanation: EXPLAIN → explanation (suffix -ation: the act of explaining)

sentence: The _____ of the project was excellent.
hint: Think about the noun form with -ment
explanation: MANAGE → management (suffix -ment: the act of managing)

sentence: We watched the _____ on television.
explanation: ADVERTISE → advertisement (suffix -ment: the result of advertising)

sentence: The _____ was very exciting. Everyone enjoyed it.
explanation: ENTERTAIN → entertainment (suffix -ment: the act of entertaining)

sentence: She felt great _____ when she passed the exam.
explanation: EXCITE → excitement (suffix -ment: the state of being excited)

sentence: His _____ made everyone in the room feel better.
explanation: KIND → kindness (suffix -ness: the quality of being kind)

sentence: _____ is very important in life.
explanation: HAPPY → happiness (suffix -ness: change -y to -i and add -ness)

sentence: The _____ of the room surprised me.
explanation: DARK → darkness (suffix -ness: the state of being dark)

sentence: She showed great _____ during the difficult time.
explanation: SAD → sadness (suffix -ness: the state of being sad)

sentence: He drives very _____. He never has accidents.
explanation: SAFE → safely (suffix -ly: in a safe manner)

sentence: She spoke _____ to the children.
explanation: SOFT → softly (suffix -ly: in a soft manner)

sentence: He finished the test _____. He was the first one done.
explanation: QUICK → quickly (suffix -ly: in a quick manner)

sentence: Please close the door _____.
explanation: QUIET → quietly (suffix -ly: in a quiet manner)

sentence: The _____ of the school starts at 9 a.m.
explanation: EDUCATE → education (suffix -tion: the process of educating)

sentence: We need to make a _____ about where to go.
explanation: DECIDE → decision (suffix -sion: the act of deciding)

sentence: The _____ of the team was very good this year.
explanation: ACHIEVE → achievement (suffix -ment: the result of achieving)

sentence: She walked _____ through the park.
explanation: SLOW → slowly (suffix -ly: in a slow manner)

sentence: The _____ of the weather changed our plans.
explanation: MOVE → movement (suffix -ment: the act of moving)

sentence: Her _____ was obvious. She could not stop smiling.
explanation: GLAD → gladness (suffix -ness: the state of being glad)

sentence: The _____ between the two countries improved.
explanation: RELATE → relation (suffix -tion: the state of relating)

sentence: He answered all the questions _____.
explanation: CORRECT → correctly (suffix -ly: in a correct manner)

sentence: The _____ of the new road will start next month.
explanation: DEVELOP → development (suffix -ment: the process of developing)

sentence: I felt great _____ when I heard the good news.
explanation: WEAK → weakness (suffix -ness: the state of being weak)
